question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R0 (CE1) changes its eBGP `remote-as` toward R2 (PE1) from 100 to 200.
R2 advertises AS 100 in OPEN; R0 expects 200. BGP OPEN fails with Bad
Peer AS NOTIFICATION. The CE1↔PE1 eBGP session never reaches
Established; R0's customer prefix 10.10.0.1/32 never enters PE1's VRF
CUSTOMER → it never gets advertised across the VPN to R4 (PE2) → R5
(CE2) loses reachability to CE1's network.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.100 (R0, CE1), the eBGP neighbor toward R2 (PE1) was reconfigured with remote-as 200 while R2 is AS 100.
R0 now expects AS 200 but R2 identifies itself as AS 100, so the two ends disagree on R2's AS and BGP cannot establish the CE1<->PE1 eBGP session. R0's customer prefix 10.10.0.1/32 never enters PE1's VRF CUSTOMER, so it is never carried across the VPN to R4 (PE2) and R5 (CE2) cannot reach CE1.
Fix: set R0's R2 (10.1.1.0) neighbor remote-as back to 100.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R0: neighbor 10.1.1.0.
- R2: neighbor 10.1.1.1.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R0 (1 instance):
- R0: Changed state to NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65001
no neighbor 10.1.1.0
!
neighbor 10.1.1.0
 remote-as 200
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy PASS in
  route-policy PASS out

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65001
no neighbor 10.1.1.0
!
neighbor 10.1.1.0
 remote-as 100
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy PASS in
  route-policy PASS out

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:20:28.075 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 10.1.1.0 Down - Remote AS configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 200)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:20:28.076 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 10.1.1.1 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: CUSTOMER) (AS: 65001)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:20:28.075 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
